content:
”We used Autotuner on Amnesiac twice. On ‘Packt Like Sardines’, I wasn’t particularly out of tune, but if you really turn up the Autotuner so it’s dead in pitch, it makes it go slightly…” he makes a nasal, depersonalised sound. “There’s also this trick you can do, which we did on both ‘Packt’ and ‘Pulk/Pull Revolving Doors’, where you give the machine a key and then you just talk into it. It desperately tries to search for the music in your speech, and produces notes at random. If you’ve assigned it a key, you’ve got music.”


EKALI Notes
--Mix in Mono
--ALWAYS Reference in Laptop Speakers

--compression on groups to glue things together and make them loud, sometimes to the point of digital clipping (you can do this with fast transients like the top layer of a kick or a snare). then I'll leave other components like organic percussion uncompressed so you end up with this loud mix with ultra-dynamic aspects

--waves s1 imager

--3 separate bongo hits where i took the attack off by adding 30ms of attack on the volume envelope and reverberated/imaged them all differently.
